visual_checks: Check the presentation's transition settings for each slide to see if automatic timing is configured
Look for timed transitions or auto-advance settings in the slide show configuration
Verify whether any slides are set to advance after a specific time interval
Check if a pause or stop mechanism is available when auto-advancing is enabled
Look for kiosk mode or looping settings that would cause uncontrolled automatic progression
pass_conditions: Slides advance only on user interaction (mouse click, keyboard press, or equivalent)
No automatic timing or timed transitions are set on any slide
If auto-advancing is used, a clearly accessible pause, stop, or manual override mechanism is provided
If the presentation loops, users can pause, stop, or control the progression
fail_conditions: One or more slides have timed transitions that auto-advance without user control
The presentation is set to auto-play or loop with no pause mechanism
Kiosk mode is enabled with timed transitions and no way for users to control the pace
Slide timing is set to advance faster than users can reasonably read the content
Auto-advancing is enabled with no option to pause, stop, or extend the time
na_conditions: The document is not a presentation format (not PowerPoint, Google Slides, or similar)
The presentation contains only a single slide
ne_conditions: The presentation's transition and timing settings could not be accessed or extracted
The slide show configuration data is not available for evaluation
common_mistakes: Setting timed transitions for a conference presentation and then publishing the same file online without removing the timing
Using auto-advance for all slides without considering users who need more time to read content
Configuring kiosk mode for a lobby display and reusing the same file for online distribution
Adding slide timing for rehearsal purposes and forgetting to remove it before publishing
Assuming that keyboard controls in presentation software are sufficient as a pause mechanism when the file may be viewed in a PDF reader or web viewer that does not support those controls

examples.pass: A presentation has all slides set to 'On Mouse Click' advancement with no automatic timing configured
examples.fail: A 20-slide presentation has each slide set to auto-advance after 5 seconds with no pause or stop mechanism, preventing users who need more reading time from accessing the content
examples.partial: Most slides advance on click but three slides in the middle of the presentation have 10-second auto-advance timers set from a prior use as a kiosk display
